types.description:
A description of mixed content (HTTP resources on HTTPS pages), as defined by
https://www.w3.org/TR/mixed-content/#categories
The security level of a page or resource.
Details about the security state of the page certificate.

properties.name: protocol
properties.description: Protocol name (e.g. "TLS 1.2" or "QUIC").

properties.name: keyExchange
properties.description: Key Exchange used by the connection, or the empty string if not applicable.

properties.name: keyExchangeGroup
properties.description: (EC)DH group used by the connection, if applicable.

properties.name: cipher
properties.description: Cipher name.

properties.name: mac
properties.description: TLS MAC. Note that AEAD ciphers do not have separate MACs.

properties.name: certificate
properties.description: Page certificate.

properties.name: subjectName
properties.description: Certificate subject name.

properties.name: issuer
properties.description: Name of the issuing CA.

properties.name: validFrom
properties.description: Certificate valid from date.

properties.name: validTo
properties.description: Certificate valid to (expiration) date

properties.name: certificateNetworkError
properties.description: The highest priority network error code, if the certificate has an error.

properties.name: certificateHasWeakSignature
properties.description: True if the certificate uses a weak signature algorithm.

properties.name: certificateHasSha1Signature
properties.description: True if the certificate has a SHA1 signature in the chain.

properties.name: modernSSL
properties.description: True if modern SSL

properties.name: obsoleteSslProtocol
properties.description: True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL protocol.

properties.name: obsoleteSslKeyExchange
properties.description: True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL key exchange.

properties.name: obsoleteSslCipher
properties.description: True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL cipher.

properties.name: obsoleteSslSignature
properties.description: True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL signature.

properties.name: safetyTipStatus
properties.description: Describes whether the page triggers any safety tips or reputation warnings. Default is unknown.

properties.name: safeUrl
properties.description: The URL the safety tip suggested ("Did you mean?"). Only filled in for lookalike matches.

types.description: Security state information about the page.

properties.name: securityState
properties.description: The security level of the page.

properties.name: certificateSecurityState
properties.description: Security state details about the page certificate.

properties.name: safetyTipInfo
properties.description: The type of Safety Tip triggered on the page. Note that this field will be set even if the Safety Tip UI was not actually shown.

properties.name: securityStateIssueIds
properties.description: Array of security state issues ids.

types.description: An explanation of an factor contributing to the security state.

properties.name: securityState
properties.description: Security state representing the severity of the factor being explained.

properties.name: title
properties.description: Title describing the type of factor.

properties.name: summary
properties.description: Short phrase describing the type of factor.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Full text explanation of the factor.

properties.name: mixedContentType
properties.description: The type of mixed content described by the explanation.

properties.name: certificate
properties.description: Page certificate.

properties.name: recommendations
properties.description: Recommendations to fix any issues.

types.description: Information about insecure content on the page.

properties.name: ranMixedContent
properties.description: Always false.

properties.name: displayedMixedContent
properties.description: Always false.

properties.name: containedMixedForm
properties.description: Always false.

properties.name: ranContentWithCertErrors
properties.description: Always false.

properties.name: displayedContentWithCertErrors
properties.description: Always false.

properties.name: ranInsecureContentStyle
properties.description: Always set to unknown.

properties.name: displayedInsecureContentStyle
properties.description: Always set to unknown.

types.description:
The action to take when a certificate error occurs. continue will continue processing the
request and cancel will cancel the request.

commands.name: setIgnoreCertificateErrors
commands.description: Enable/disable whether all certificate errors should be ignored.

parameters.name: ignore
parameters.description: If true, all certificate errors will be ignored.

commands.name: handleCertificateError
commands.description: Handles a certificate error that fired a certificateError event.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The action to take on the certificate error.

commands.name: setOverrideCertificateErrors
commands.description:
Enable/disable overriding certificate errors. If enabled, all certificate error events need to
be handled by the DevTools client and should be answered with `handleCertificateError` commands.

parameters.name: override
parameters.description: If true, certificate errors will be overridden.

events.name: certificateError
events.description:
There is a certificate error. If overriding certificate errors is enabled, then it should be
handled with the `handleCertificateError` command. Note: this event does not fire if the
certificate error has been allowed internally. Only one client per target should override
certificate errors at the same time.

events.name: visibleSecurityStateChanged
events.description: The security state of the page changed.

parameters.name: visibleSecurityState
parameters.description: Security state information about the page.

events.name: securityStateChanged
events.description: The security state of the page changed. No longer being sent.

parameters.name: schemeIsCryptographic
parameters.description: True if the page was loaded over cryptographic transport such as HTTPS.

parameters.name: explanations
parameters.description:
Previously a list of explanations for the security state. Now always
empty.

parameters.name: insecureContentStatus
parameters.description: Information about insecure content on the page.

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: Overrides user-visible description of the state. Always omitted.
